methods.description: Statistical procedures were applied to analyze outcome variables and covariates, comparing different product groups (overall AIT, non-AIT control, natural SLIT, natural SCIT, and four individual allergoid SCIT formulations). Analyses included regression models and survival analysis, with all tests being two-sided and a significance level of P < 0.05. Analyses were performed using SAS 9.4 software.

steps.description: Descriptive statistics were presented for all outcome variables and covariates, split by grouped or individual products (ie, overall AIT, non-AIT control, natural SLIT, natural SCIT and four individual allergoid SCIT formulations).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate descriptive statistics for outcome variables and covariates, grouped by product type
outputs: descriptive statistics (e.g., mean, standard deviation, counts)

steps.description: Analyses of medication intake progression (AR or asthma) were carried out by regression using a general linear model, with the ratio of annual number of prescriptions in the analysis period vs the pre-index period used as the outcome variable.
steps.searchable_summary: perform general linear model regression on the ratio of annual prescriptions
inputs: ratio of annual prescriptions (analysis period / pre-index period)
covariates (implicitly, based on general analysis description)
outputs: general linear model results (e.g., coefficients, p-values)

steps.description: Analysis of asthma medication intake as a Y/N variable was achieved by logistic regression. Because the probability of asthma medication intake occurrence would also depend on length of analytical time span, the individual length of this period was included as a covariate.
steps.searchable_summary: perform logistic regression on binary asthma medication intake, including analysis time span as a covariate
outputs: logistic regression results (e.g., odds ratios, p-values)

steps.description: Time to asthma medication intake was investigated using survival analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: perform survival analysis on time to asthma medication intake
outputs: survival analysis results (e.g., Kaplan-Meier curves, hazard ratios, log-rank test p-value)

steps.description: The proportion of patients with any level of treatment between the AIT and non-AIT control groups was also analysed by logistic regression.
steps.searchable_summary: perform logistic regression to compare the proportion of patients with any treatment between two groups
inputs: treatment status (any level of treatment Y/N)
outputs: logistic regression results (e.g., odds ratio for group difference, p-value)
